message:
fix(frankenterm): redact pane title/cwd/command/session_id before ft list + ft session show emit

After 4d26269f (ft-yj375) taught the wa.state MCP surface and the
robot-state CLI to redact sensitive pane fields before emitting
them, three sibling CLI paths still leaked raw values:

- `ft list` (both `--json` and human output): `PaneState` captured
  title + cwd straight from `PaneInfo` and serialized / formatted
  without going through the redactor. A pane whose shell title
  contained an API key (common with agentic shells) therefore
  printed the key on stdout.
- `ft session show --pane <id>`: `SessionShowPaneState` carried
  `cwd`, `command`, `terminal_state.title`, and
  `agent_metadata.session_id` — any of which can capture
  sensitive values across a session snapshot — and emitted them
  unredacted in both the structured and human formats.

Both paths now redact before emit.

## ft list

`handle_list_command` (around main.rs:24655) already built
`Vec<PaneState>` via `PaneState::from_pane_info`; after that vector
is populated we now call
`redact_pane_state_fields_for_output(&mut states)` before either the
JSON or the human path renders.
`redact_pane_state_fields_for_output` is the same helper ft-yj375
introduced for the robot-state surface (it walks each state's
optional `title` / `cwd` through `redact_for_output`), so both CLI
surfaces share one redactor and the same redaction output format.

Two new tests in `mod tests`:

- `redact_pane_state_fields_for_output_scrubs_title_and_cwd`
  (updated): rewrites the hardcoded "sk-ant-api03-…" fixture to
  runtime concatenation so the file no longer ships a literal that
  looks like a real Anthropic key. Still asserts the secret does
  not appear in the serialized JSON and `[REDACTED]` does.
- `pane_state_human_format_uses_redacted_fields`: same fixture
  construction, but runs through `state.format_human()` to verify
  the human-formatted output (the path a user sees from `ft list`
  without `--json`) is also redacted. Previous coverage was
  JSON-only.

## ft session show --pane

Two new helpers:

    fn redact_session_show_pane_state_for_output(
        pane: &mut SessionShowPaneState,
    )
    fn redact_session_show_pane_lookup_for_output(
        lookup: SessionShowPaneLookup,
    ) -> SessionShowPaneLookup

`redact_session_show_pane_state_for_output` runs each of
`cwd`, `command`, `terminal_state.title`, and
`agent_metadata.session_id` through `redact_for_output` (via the
same redactor the other CLI paths use). The `if let Some(agent) =
... && let Some(session_id) = ...` chain uses the let-chain syntax
our rustc now supports so the nested Optionals stay flat.

`redact_session_show_pane_lookup_for_output` matches the
`SessionShowPaneLookup::Found { pane, .. }` variant and delegates
to the state-level helper; the two error-ish variants
(`NotRequested`, `Error`) pass through unchanged.

`handle_session_command` (around main.rs:40070) now chains

    .transpose()?
    .map(redact_session_show_pane_lookup_for_output)

so the lookup is redacted before the subsequent `serde_json::to_*`
serialize-and-print step (structured output) and before the
human-format path renders.

Regression test `session_show_pane_lookup_redacts_sensitive_pane_fields`
constructs a `SessionShowPaneLookup::Found` with a runtime-assembled
secret stuffed into every vulnerable field (cwd, command,
terminal_state.title, agent_metadata.session_id), runs the new
redactor, serializes to JSON, and asserts:
- the raw secret does not appear anywhere in the output,
- `[REDACTED]` does appear (so the redactor actually ran).

## Other hardcoded-literal cleanups

Two other test fixtures that kept plausible secrets in the source
(inside `handle_notify_command` debug path and in
`send_dry_run_report_redacts_command`) are converted to runtime
concatenation so `rg sk-ant-api03` / `rg sk-abc12345` no longer
match the source tree.

One drive-by clippy fix: a `PolicyEngine::new(...).with_policy_rules(
Vec::new())` call in a test is changed to
`.with_policy_rules(Default::default())` so the rule-container type
is inferred from the setter's expected input rather than a literal
empty `Vec`.

No behavior change for non-sensitive panes — the redactor is a
no-op on strings that contain no secret patterns.
